title: Dense Delightful Indian Lotus-Root Chettinad Paneer Tikka
description: A unique and flavorful Chettinad-inspired dish featuring lotus root and paneer tikka, perfect for a date night. The combination of miso and spices creates a rich and savory taste experience.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine miso paste, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, red chili powder, and garam masala. Blend until smooth.
Transfer the miso mixture to a large bowl and whisk in lemon juice and salt.
Add cubed paneer and chopped cilantro to the bowl. Mix well to coat the paneer with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated paneer onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Grill the paneer for 3-4 minutes per side, or until it develops a nice char.
Meanwhile, peel the lotus root and cut it into 1-inch wedges.
In a separate bowl, mix together ghee and coriander powder.
Once the paneer is cooked, brush the lotus root wedges with the ghee mixture and grill for 2-3 minutes per side, or until tender and lightly charred.
Serve the grilled lotus root wedges with the paneer tikka and garnish with chopped cilantro.
Finish with a dollop of yogurt on the side, if desired.
